input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework watches `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and swaps values automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent — `SetAppThemeColor` / `SetAppTheme`:**

```csharp
// For Color-typed properties (shorthand helper)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Colors.White);

// For any property type (generic version)
myButton.SetAppTheme<Color>(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));

// Works with non-color properties too
myPage.SetAppTheme<string>(Page.TitleProperty,
    light: "Light Mode",
    dark:  "Dark Mode");
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Tracks the device's system-wide light/dark setting |
| **Updates** | Automatic — values swap in real time when the user toggles the OS theme |
| **Scope** | Per-property; apply it to every property you want themed |
| **Resources** | For app-wide consistency, define themed colors in `App.xaml` `AppThemeBinding` resources and reference them with `{StaticResource}` |

This approach requires no custom renderers, no manual event subscriptions, and works on all MAUI-supported platforms.
